statement.markdown: Implement a function to validate whether a given string is a pangram. A pangram is a sentence that uses all the letters of a given alphabet at least once. For this problem, consider the English alphabet and the given sentence will only contain lowercase letters. If the sentence uses all unique letters of the alphabet, return True; otherwise, return False. Consider edge cases where the input sentence may be empty or contain only unique letters.
statement.inputFormat: A string containing lowercase letters only.
statement.outputFormat: Return True if the string is a pangram, False otherwise.
statement.notes: This problem is to fix the bug in the provided code.

examples.explanation: The string contains all unique letters of the alphabet.
The string does not contain all unique letters of the alphabet.

hints.text: The current implementation has a logic error causing incorrect outputs for certain edge cases.

editorial.title: Correct Implementation of Pangram Validation
editorial.intuition: The correct approach involves comparing the set of unique letters in the input string with the entire English alphabet.
editorial.bruteForce: The incorrect implementation attempts to compare each character of the input string with the entire alphabet, resulting in inefficient time complexity.
editorial.optimalSolution: Compare the set of unique letters in the input string with the entire English alphabet.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: If the set of unique letters in the input string is a superset of the entire alphabet, then the string is a pangram.

testCases.input: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
